message:
🌱 Add traefik: Feature request : SSL/TLS certificate status in web UI mission (#1984)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "traefik-6156-feature-request-ssl-tls-certificate-status-in-web-ui",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "traefik: Feature request : SSL/TLS certificate status in web UI",
+    "description": "Feature request : SSL/TLS certificate status in web UI. Requested by 48+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current traefik deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your traefik version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik\nhelm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working traefik installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review traefik configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik\nkubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik\n```\nI'd love to see SSL/TLS certificate status in the web UI. For example:\n- see each domain's certificate\n- status of each certificate, date of last renewal, expiry\n- any to messages pertinent to the certificate's renewal.\n\nThanks for all the work!"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Feature request : SSL/TLS certificate status in web UI",
+        "description": "At least partially\n\n### What does this PR do?\n\nThis PR creates a dedicated view for certificate information. I locally already have more versions, which would allow renewal or revocation / deletion of ACME certificates, but the changes are too many and too wild. So I thought, maybe first start with showing some information.\n\nIt was more difficult than I thought. Reason one is that I am not so familiar with go and vite. The second \"issue\" I had is that a certificate does not have a key to\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade traefik to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade traefik:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade traefik traefik/traefik --namespace traefik\n```\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n traefik\nhelm list -n traefik\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik\nkubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Feature request : SSL/TLS certificate status in web UI\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "At least partially\n\n### What does this PR do?\n\nThis PR creates a dedicated view for certificate information. I locally already have more versions, which would allow renewal or revocation / deletion of ACME certificates, but the changes are too many and too wild. So I thought, maybe first start with showing some information.\n\nIt was more difficult than I thought.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },

+    },
